content:
1 min read
1 hour ago

--

I’ve explored many different stacks over the years but have pretty much settled now on the stack I’m describing in this article.
I love Go’s philosophy of keeping things simple and have applied this principle to the whole stack.
The backend will be purely Go and where possible using the Go standard library so there are zero dependencies on 3rd party packages.
The front end will be HTMX, Tailwind CSS and Alpine.js with the goal of writing as little Javascript as possible and with no build step required.
All my apps will be PWA’s and designed for mobile, web or desktop.
Database will be SQlite for simple apps and PostgreSQL for more complex data requirments.
Where possible I will use Go’s embed package and embed everything (except the database lol) into a single executable and run it in a container on Goggle Cloud run (or similar service on other cloud providers) or on a hosted service that provides bare metal.
I’m still learning (or refreshing myself) on these front-end technologies but from my experiments this an elegant solution with many pro’s and very few cons, for my set of requirments.
For static web sites I will use Hugo or Framer deployed to Cloudflare Pages.
I’ll write another post on tooling and dev environment but for now would be interested in your comments and suggestions.
